title: athlete-guardian — model relationships
description: One owned entity: AthleteGuardian. Pivot between Tenant + Athlete + User. Cascades organization + region + branch through athlete → branch. Cascades application through tenant. No `application_id`, `organization_id`, `region_id`, `branch_id`, or `scope_node_id` — forbidden columns per tenancy-columns.md §5. Every outbound FK stays same-tenant per defense-in-depth.

outbound.description: Every guardian row is tenant-scoped per tenancy-columns.md §3. Cascade delete on TenantErased via PurgeAthleteGuardianDataForErasedTenant hook.

The athlete this row grants guardianship over. CASCADE — guardian rows have no meaning without their athlete. When an Athlete is hard-deleted (past 7-year retention), guardian rows go with it. IMMUTABLE post-create — a guardian belongs to one athlete forever.

The guardian's login. RESTRICT — the User cannot be soft-deleted while they hold an active guardianship. The guardian handoff flow (transfer guardianship to another User) must complete first. Enforced by PreventUserArchiveWithActiveGuardianship hook.
The HR User who verified this guardian (typically staff — HR role). SET NULL on User archive so the audit trail survives while the reference nulls.
The User who filed the dispute. SET NULL for audit trail preservation.
The User who filed the revocation. SET NULL for audit trail preservation.
Wave 4+ storage integration. RESTRICT — signed verification documents cannot be hard-deleted while referenced. 7-year retention aligned with the guardian row.

inbound.description: Downstream modules (Wave 3+) reach AthleteGuardian through the cross-module rule + resolver — never a direct FK from another table (except the polymorphic morph on notifications).
examples: athlete::AthleteObserver — for minor athletes, consent_recorded_by_user_id MUST resolve to a User with an active AthleteGuardian row on the athlete + has_legal_custody=true (Wave 3b hard-fail via consent_recorded_by_authorised_guardian rule)
athlete-enrollment::AthleteEnrollment (Wave 3a sibling) — enrollment approval routes through the primary guardian's User for consent on provisional enrollments
notifications::NotificationRoutingHook (Wave 4+) — routes athlete-adjacent notifications to guardians with can_receive_communications=true
safeguarding::MedicalClearance (Wave 4+) — reads guardians with can_authorise_medical_care=true for emergency medical decisions
inbound.onDelete: N/A — no inbound FKs to guardian rows.

accessor_chain.description: The AthleteGuardian model delegates presentation accessors to its athlete + user for uniform display.

AthleteGuardian.is_active: verification_status IN ('verified', 'pending', 'disputed') AND deleted_at IS NULL

notes: AthleteGuardian carries `application_id` INDIRECTLY via its Tenant. Enforced by tenancy-compliance-auditor.
AthleteGuardian cascades organization + region + branch through its Athlete → Branch. `guardian.organization_id`, `guardian.region_id`, `guardian.branch_id` DO NOT EXIST — forbidden columns.
AthleteGuardian.athlete_id + user_id are IMMUTABLE post-create. Re-pointing is an audit-trail launder; the correct path is remove + re-add.
AthleteGuardian.tenant_id is IMMUTABLE. Cross-tenant guardian moves are forbidden.

The `is_primary` flag is transaction-atomic — the /set-primary route promotes-and-demotes in one DB transaction. Concurrent flips are caught by the partial unique index + observer's SELECT ... FOR UPDATE.

There is NO `Parent` / `ParentLink` model. AthleteGuardian IS the parent-child link — the naming reflects the domain-agnostic scope (a legal guardian may not be a parent; a foster parent + court-appointed guardian all fit).
